Records.authors: Anastasiou, Charalampos, Beerli, Stefan, Bucherer, Stefan, Daleo, Alejandro, Kunszt, Zoltan
Records.description: Parent topology TP1 for two-loop $gg \to H$ via massive quark loop. Left triangle is massless ($k_1$-loop), right box is massive ($k_2$-loop with mass $m_t$), connected by massive propagator $(k_1-k_2)^2-m_t^2$. Hosts 10 of the 17 master integrals. Results in HPLs with alphabet $\{x,1-x,1+x\}$ where $x=(\sqrt{1-4m_t^2/s}-1)/(\sqrt{1-4m_t^2/s}+1)$.

Two-loop planar double-box family for $gg \to HH$ with 4 massive ($m_t$) and 3 massless internal propagators. Same graph topology as $D_6$ but with different mass assignment: the rung $(k_1-k_2)^2$ is massive while propagators $k_1^2, (k_1-p_1-p_2)^2, (k_1-p_1)^2$ are massless. Expanded in small $m_t^2$ for the amplitude at $\mathcal{O}(m_t^{16})$. One crossing family: $D_{26} = D_{20}(p_4,p_3,p_1,p_2)$. Total 12 master integrals across $D_{20}$ and $D_{26}$. Integration measure: $(\mu^2)^{(4-d)/2} e^{\varepsilon \gamma_E} d^d k / (i \pi^{d/2})$.

Records.familyName: PLA / massive 2-loop 4-point planar double box A
Records.authors: Becchetti, Matteo, Bonciani, Roberto, Cieri, Leandro, Coro, Federico, Ripani, Federico
Records.location: Table 1 (propagator definitions), Section 3.1 (general setup), Section 3.2 (DEQs), Eqs. (15)-(18) (alphabet and roots), Appendix A (MI list)
Records.description: Planar double-box integral family PLA for two-loop $q\bar{q} \to \gamma\gamma$ form factors with massive top quark loop. The massless $k_1$-loop (3 propagators) forms one box and the massive $k_2$-loop (4 propagators with $m_t$) forms the adjacent box. 32 master integrals, differential equations in canonical logarithmic form with 21-letter alphabet containing 5 algebraically independent square roots (not simultaneously rationalizable). Solved semi-analytically via generalised power series expansion (DiffExp). Boundary conditions: all MIs vanish at $y=z=0$ except $f_1=1$ and $f_2$ (known analytically). Numerical validation against AMFlow to 200 digits. Corresponds to diagrams (a) in Fig. 1 where top quark couples to external photons.

Two-loop four-point planar double-box with massive top loop (propagators $x_1, x_2, x_3, x_7$ carry mass $m$) and massless bottom loop. The seven-fold maximal cut is performed in Baikov variables and yields a logarithmic result at order $\varepsilon^0$; the order-$\varepsilon$ term is given for the numerator integral $F_{\text{double-box-N}}$ (with $x_8$ in the numerator) in Eq. (A.18) in terms of weight-2 logarithms. All four external legs are massless.

First of three planar two-loop four-point integral families (called PLA in the paper) used for di-jet and photon-plus-jet production via a heavy-quark loop. The top sector is a planar double box in which the right box runs entirely through the massive quark while the left box is massless, with one massless rung in the middle. Defined by 9 denominators (7 genuine propagators plus 2 ISPs). Contributes to the full 220 master-integral basis computed in epsilon-factorized form; the system closes on multiple polylogarithms plus two elliptic kernels (holomorphic period and an extra G-function). Complete analytic results through $\mathcal{O}(\varepsilon^0)$ of the two-loop amplitudes are provided, with differential equations and canonical basis in the accompanying Zenodo repository.
